summary: Locked in the two ConfigError code paths that the prior consumer-error-messages slice implemented but did not assert. Added two new tests to RepoAutomationConsumerAdoptionSmokeTests: (1) git-not-on-PATH constructs an isolated bin with only a python3 symlink and asserts the supervisor surfaces 'git executable not found on PATH' with no Traceback; (2) malformed-JSON queue writes '{invalid json,' to the consumer's automation/queue/slices.json and asserts 'invalid JSON in' + the queue path in the friendly message. The smoke test class now has 8 tests; the full repo_automation_sync suite has 17; the full automation-check ran 118 tests OK.

consumer_failure_modes_now_smoke_tested.observed: stop: git executable not found on PATH: <FileNotFoundError text>\nhint: install Git, or ensure git is on PATH for this process.

scope_observed_from: automation/supervisor/policy.py (load_json catch on json.JSONDecodeError; git_dirty_paths catch on FileNotFoundError)
gaps_named_for_follow_up: Workflow templates in the manifest so consumers get a CI scaffold during bootstrap (not queued; user-prioritized boundary).
Prompt-fragment override portability proof (not queued; user-prioritized boundary).
Real third-party repo migration when a target is named explicitly by the user.
not_claimed: Every ConfigError emit site is now asserted. The four asserted paths are: missing queue file (supervisor + context-builder, 2 tests), not-a-git-repo (1 test), git-not-on-PATH (1 test, new), and malformed JSON (1 test, new). The 'git command failed' generic branch in git_dirty_paths is not asserted; that's a defensive branch unreachable through the normal consumer setup.
A real external repository has experienced these failure modes. The smoke test bootstraps temp consumers only.
residual_risks: The git-not-on-PATH test relies on shutil.which('python3') resolving to a working interpreter. If a runner has python3 only under a name like python3.13, the test skips with a clear message rather than failing. The test also leaks the symlink into self.tmpdir, which addCleanup tears down.
The malformed-JSON assertion is substring-based ('invalid JSON in' + the queue path). Editing the policy.ConfigError message text will require updating the asserted substring.
recommended_next_reason: Queue is empty. Remaining named boundaries are workflow-templates-in-manifest, prompt-fragment-override-portability, and real-third-party-migration; none queued.
